summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard captures a tight May ’77 show highlighted by a monster Not Fade Away and smoking Sugaree
review: The recording is a pristine sound‑board capture that sounds “crisp”, “A+” and virtually hiss‑free, delivering every nuance of the band’s playing. Musically the concert is solid throughout – a well‑sequenced first set followed by a second set that shines with a powerful Not Fade Away > GDTRFB stretch, a tight Terrapin > Estimated > Eyes of the World, and a monster Sugaree, with Garcia’s guitar and Mickey Hart’s drums getting special praise.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive 4‑5 star reviews (≈90% of entries) highlight both excellent soundboard quality and strong musical performance; only a single low‑star outlier exists.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Repeatedly described as crystal clear, super crisp, A+, beautiful sounding with no hiss or distortion – a textbook Betty Board capture.

standout_songs: Terrapin > Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is praised as a solid, nicely paced collection; second set builds momentum with a strong Samson intro, gentle High Time and then a soaring Not Fade Away into GDTRFB, followed by tight jams (Terrapin/Estimated/Eyes) that keep the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is called perfect, his solo on Bertha ‘blisters’, he “steals the show”, and his noodling during NFA is described as inspired and slinky.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass gets noted for an opening cannon‑shot, dynamic interplay with Jerry on Eyes of the World, and generally solid playing (though one reviewer says he’s “all over the place”).
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir receives kudos for strong vocal work, a great tag‑along jam during NFA/Drums, and being described as ‘fantastic’ throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart is praised for “smashing” drums, tight GDTRFB segment, and energetic drumming that drives the NFA jam.
